rulings.Case Title: 690 MARKET CLUB OWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. ROBERT L. GARCIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 2 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF 690 MARKET CLUB OWNERS ASSOCIATION HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. Denied without prejudice. Defendant submits a financial statement showing income of $9,293.46 per month and expenses of $6,385.23 per month. Although Defendant included a notation stating the funds from the adoptive aid program will end in February 2015, Defendant does not reflect this information in the financial statement. The Court cannot make a determination based on an outdated financial statement as to whether Defendant is entitled to a claim of exemption. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION et al VS. THE DOCTORS' COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Vacate Or Correct Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 2 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION, KAROL MEIER, SANDI SELIG-FARNEY Petition To Vacate Or Correct Arbitration Award. The motion to confirm the arbitration award is granted and the motion to vacate is denied as moot. The "Final Award of Dismissal and Award of Costs" does not list Mr. Bradshaw and it is clear that he was not a party to the arbitration. The fact that the order granting the dispositive motion referred to Mr. Bradshaw does not change that finding. The complaint in this case indicates that Mt. Tam et. al.'s only claim related to the Cumis issue. Mt. Tam et. al. fail to clearly highlight what other issues the panel was supposed to arbitrate. There was no reservation of rights letter. Mr. Bradshaw even admitted that fact before the arbitrators. TDC stated that it would defend the action, but indemnity would not be available if plaintiff established some of its allegations. In other words, TDC was stating that indemnity/coverage would be denied if certain facts were established. There was no reservation of rights. Under CC 2860(b), there is no Cumis issue where coverage is denied. Mt. Tam et al. speculates that the Goldman communications would have made a difference on the Cumis issue. Mt. Tam et. al. does not meet the substantial prejudice standard of CCP 1286.2(a)(5). Mt. Tam et. al. does not demonstrate that the arbitration panels' use of the dispositive motion procedure was improper. The award should not be vacated on public policy grounds. There is no public policy violation where an arbitration panel finds, as a factual matter, that an insured is not entitled to Cumis counsel. This case does not present a public policy violation or illegality that warrants judicial scrutiny of an arbitration award. The court's prior ruling in the Goldman case, which is on appeal and not final, does not affect these proceedings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KARMA QUICK VS. JONATHAN FRIED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Karma Quick'S Renewed Motion To Compel And Request For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 2, 2015, Line 2, Plaintiff Karma Quick's Renewed Motion To Compel And Request For Terminating Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalonotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is GRANTED in its entirety. Plaintiff's counsel shall submit a proposed order in Word format by 4pm today to pcc@manningllp.com The Court will sign a slightly modified order at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IGOR KRAYCHIK VS. EXIGEN (USA), INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition Of Defendants To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 2 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT EXIGEN INSURANCE SOLUTIONS, INC., EXIGEN ELITE, INC., EXIGEN HOLDINGS, INC., EXIGEN MORTAGE ASSET MANAGEMENT, INC., EXIGEN SERVICES , INC., EXIGEN, LTD., EXIGEN PROPERTIES, INC, EXIFAST, Petition Of Defendants To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action. Petition granted as to Defendants Exigen Insurance Solutions, Inc. and Exigen Elite, Inc. Petition denied as moot as to Defendant Exigen Mortgage Asset Management, Inc. in light of the stipulation to withdraw the petition. A nonsignatory defendant may invoke an arbitration clause to compel a signatory plaintiff to arbitrate its claims when the causes of action against the nonsignatory are "intimately founded in and intertwined" with the underlying contract obligations. (Boucher v. Alliance Title Company, Inc. (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 262, 271.) By relying on contract terms in its claims against Defendants, Plaintiff is equitably estopped from repudiating the arbitration clause contained in that agreement. (Id. at 272.) Furthermore, Plaintiff has produced insufficient evidence to establish that the arbitration agreement was superseded and nullified. Additionally, the agreement is not unconscionable. The evidence presented establishes that the employment contract was negotiable as to job title, bonus, salary, moving allowance, and start date (Kraychik Decl., Ex. 1), and Plaintiff failed to present evidence that any other provision in the Offer Letter was not similarly negotiable. Furthermore, a failure to include or provide the arbitration rules referenced in the agreement alone is insufficient to support a finding of procedural unconscionability where the rules affect only Plaintiff's procedural rights. (Peng v. First Republic Bank (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1472.) Further, the arbitration agreement is not substantively unconscionable. Plaintiff's argument as to lack of mutuality lacks merit because the agreement specifically limits its application to the employment relationship between Plaintiff and the signatory Defendant only, and the Confidentiality and Invention Assignment Agreement alone does not compel a finding that the agreement lacks mutuality. Further, the qualifying language in the attorneys' fees provision keeps it from running afoul of Armendariz's minimum requirement that "the arbitration agreement ... cannot generally require the employee to bear any type of expense that the employee would not be required to bear if he or she were free to bring the action in court." (Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83, 110-11.) Additionally, a mere possibility of the "repeat player effect"-without any evidence-is not enough to render an arbitration agreement unconscionable, and the agreement is not subject to unilateral modification or revocation. Defendants' request to stay the action pending completion of the arbitration proceedings is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

In his opposition, Plaintiff states that he wishes to file an amended complaint. Plaintiff is entitled to amend his complaint once as a matter of course. (CCP sec. 472.) rulings.Case Title: JMA INVESTMENTS VS. MOUNTAIN WEST FINANCIAL, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 2 2015, Line 13, DEFENDANT MT HAWLEY INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. Res Judicata does not apply because the two actions do not involve the same causes of action. The court recognizes that collateral estoppel may apply. The court further recognizes that the federal judgment could be deemed final under federal law. In an abundance of caution and to conserve resources, however, the court will abate this action per CCP 128(a)(8) until the federal action is resolved. CCP 430.10(c) does not apply because the two actions do not involve the same cause of action and CCP 597 does not apply because that abatement statute requires the matter to be raised as a defense. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: AMTRUST NORTH AMERICA, INC VS. SQUARETRADE, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And (1) Petition To Compel Arbitration And (2) Motion For Stay Pending Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 2 2015, Line 14, DEFENDANT SQUARETRADE, INC. Notice And (1) Petition To Compel Arbitration And (2) Motion For Stay Pending Arbitration. The motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. Plaintiff is correct that the arbitration clause is narrow and the pending arbitration only pertains to 11 claims. P's complaint, however, is pleaded so generally that it also includes those arbitrable claims. The court grants this motion and stays the action because the same claims are implicated. The stay will not unduly prejudice the plaintiff. The arbitration agreement provides for an expedited arbitration. In addition, resolution of the arbitration may have ripple effects on this case and help lead to a quicker resolution of the court action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
